question.text: The indemnification provision in an IMA for Arjun Growth Fund specifies that the investment manager shall be indemnified by the fund for losses arising from:

answer.text: Fraud or willful misconduct by the investment manager
Any and all losses regardless of the cause
Actions taken in good faith and in accordance with the IMA, excluding gross negligence
Regulatory penalties imposed by SEBI for violations committed by the manager

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Standard IMA indemnification covers the manager for actions taken in good faith within the scope of the IMA, explicitly excluding fraud, willful misconduct, and gross negligence. Blanket indemnification would be unconscionable, and indemnifying against regulatory penalties for the manager's own violations would be against public policy. **The Trap:** The option for any and all losses seems protective of the manager but no reasonable investor or legal counsel would agree to unlimited indemnification — carve-outs for misconduct are industry standard. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series VIII (Equity Derivatives) covers indemnification in broker-client agreements with similar carve-out principles. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Review the indemnification clause carefully — overly broad manager indemnification shifts risk disproportionately to investors and signals poor governance.
